title: What are other message types in AMHS similar FPL?

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: What are other message types in AMHS similar FPL?

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
Other message types in AMHS similar to FPL are:

1. CLA - ICAO Flight Plan Amendment/Cancellation Messages
2. DEP - Departure Message
3. ARR - Arrival Message
4. CHG - Change Message
5. DLA - Delay Message
6. EST - Estimate Message
7. PBN - Performance Based Navigation Message
8. RQS - Request Message
9. CNL - Cancellation Message
10. DIV - Diversion Message

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Provide format for FPL, ARR, CHG , DLA and CNL and explain

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
1. FPL (Flight Plan Message)
The format for FPL message in AMHS is as follows:

MESSAGE TYPE: FPL
MESSAGE SEQUENCE NUMBER:
MESSAGE TYPE ADDITIONAL INDICATORS: NIL
REFERENCE NUMBER:
MESSAGE ORIGINATOR: (ICAO 8-letter location indicator or AFTN address)
MESSAGE RECIPIENT: (ICAO 8-letter location indicator or AFTN address)
TIME OF MESSAGE: (UTC time)
FLIGHT PLAN STRIP/SERIAL NUMBER: (Alpha-numeric identifier assigned by the originator)
MESSAGE TEXT: (Contains the entire flight plan contents in a specific format as per ICAO regulations)

The FPL message is used to convey the details of a proposed flight to all the relevant air traffic service units along the intended route of flight. The message helps ATC to plan and manage air traffic in a coordinated and efficient manner.

2. ARR (Arrival Message)
The format for ARR message in AMHS is as follows:

MESSAGE TYPE: ARR
MESSAGE SEQUENCE NUMBER:
MESSAGE TYPE ADDITIONAL INDICATORS: NIL
REFERENCE NUMBER:
MESSAGE ORIGINATOR: (ICAO 8-letter location indicator or AFTN address)
MESSAGE RECIPIENT: (ICAO 8-letter location indicator or AFTN address)
TIME OF MESSAGE: (UTC time)
FLIGHT ID: (Identification of the aircraft)
MESSAGE TEXT: (Contains the details about the arrival of the aircraft at the airport, such as estimated time of arrival, parking stand allocation, etc.)

ARR message is used to provide the airport authorities with the necessary information about the arrival of an aircraft at the airport, so that they can prepare the necessary ground handling services.

3. CHG (Change Message)
The format for CHG message in AMHS is as follows:

MESSAGE TYPE: CHG
MESSAGE SEQUENCE NUMBER:
MESSAGE TYPE ADDITIONAL INDICATORS: NIL
REFERENCE NUMBER:
MESSAGE ORIGINATOR: (ICAO 8-letter location indicator or AFTN address)
MESSAGE RECIPIENT: (ICAO 8-letter location indicator or AFTN address)
TIME OF MESSAGE: (UTC time)
FLIGHT ID: (Identification of the aircraft)
MESSAGE TEXT: (Contains the details about the changes made to the flight plan, such as change in route, altitude, destination, etc.)

The CHG message is used to inform the ATC units along the route of flight about any changes that have been made to the original FPL.

4. DLA (Delay Message)
The format for DLA message in AMHS is as follows:

MESSAGE TYPE: DLA
MESSAGE SEQUENCE NUMBER:
MESSAGE TYPE ADDITIONAL INDICATORS: NIL
REFERENCE NUMBER:
MESSAGE ORIGINATOR: (ICAO 8-letter location indicator or AFTN address)
MESSAGE RECIPIENT: (ICAO 8-letter location indicator or AFTN address)
TIME OF MESSAGE: (UTC time)
FLIGHT ID: (Identification of the aircraft)
MESSAGE TEXT: (Contains the details of the delay, such as the reason for delay, expected time of departure, etc.)

DLA message is used to report any delay that is likely to affect the air traffic flow to the destination airport.

5. CNL (Cancellation Message)
The format for CNL message in AMHS is as follows:

MESSAGE TYPE: CNL
MESSAGE SEQUENCE NUMBER:
MESSAGE TYPE ADDITIONAL INDICATORS: NIL
REFERENCE NUMBER:
MESSAGE ORIGINATOR: (ICAO 8-letter location indicator or AFTN address)
MESSAGE RECIPIENT: (ICAO 8-letter location indicator or AFTN address)
TIME OF MESSAGE: (UTC time)
FLIGHT ID: (Identification of the aircraft)
MESSAGE TEXT: (Provides the reason for the cancellation of the flight)

The CNL message is used to inform the ATC units that a particular flight has been cancelled for a specific reason. The message helps the ATC units to take necessary action to manage the air traffic flow.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Provide format and example for FPL, ARR, CHG , DLA and CNL and explain

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
1. FPL (Flight Plan Message)
The format for FPL message in AMHS is as follows:

MESSAGE TYPE: FPL
MESSAGE SEQUENCE NUMBER: 123456
MESSAGE TYPE ADDITIONAL INDICATORS: NIL
REFERENCE NUMBER: ABC123
MESSAGE ORIGINATOR: EGLLZTZX
MESSAGE RECIPIENT: EDDFZTZX
TIME OF MESSAGE: 121500Z
FLIGHT PLAN STRIP/SERIAL NUMBER: 12345A
MESSAGE TEXT: 
FPL-EDDF1100-EGLL1100
-B738/M-SDE2E3FGHIJ3J5M1P1RWXYZ/LB1D1
-EDDS0300-N0434F330 LISBA DCT
-ADENU UL603 DIMAL DCT SUMUM/N0408F350 UZ738
-DIDOR UN858 VESAN UZ10 BOMBI NIKOL1A-EGLL1425
-PBN/A1B2C3D4O1S2
-REG/GBXZZ
-EET/EDVV0024 EBUR0054 LFFF0151 EGTT0221
-SEL/JLDANGER

The FPL message is used to convey the details of a proposed flight to all the relevant air traffic service units along the intended route of flight. In this example, the flight is from Frankfurt (EDDF) to London Heathrow (EGLL), using a Boeing 737-800 aircraft with various routing information and performance-based navigation information.

2. ARR (Arrival Message)
The format for ARR message in AMHS is as follows:

MESSAGE TYPE: ARR
MESSAGE SEQUENCE NUMBER: 234567
MESSAGE TYPE ADDITIONAL INDICATORS: NIL
REFERENCE NUMBER: XYZ456
MESSAGE ORIGINATOR: EGLLZTZX
MESSAGE RECIPIENT: EDDFZTZX
TIME OF MESSAGE: 143000Z
FLIGHT ID: BA456
MESSAGE TEXT: 
ARR-BA456-EGLL
-PTS/PARKINGSTAND1234
-ETA/1435
-PBN/A1B2C3D4O1S2

ARR message is used to provide the airport authorities with the necessary information about the arrival of an aircraft at the airport. In this example, flight BA456 is arriving at London Heathrow (EGLL) and is expected to arrive at 1435.

3. CHG (Change Message)
The format for CHG message in AMHS is as follows:

MESSAGE TYPE: CHG
MESSAGE SEQUENCE NUMBER: 345678
MESSAGE TYPE ADDITIONAL INDICATORS: NIL
REFERENCE NUMBER: LMN789
MESSAGE ORIGINATOR: EGLLZTZX
MESSAGE RECIPIENT: EDDFZTZX
TIME OF MESSAGE: 150000Z
FLIGHT ID: BA456
MESSAGE TEXT: 
CHG-BA456-EGLL
-ROUTE/PTASO UN491 GIMER DCT
-ATC/K1234

The CHG message is used to inform the ATC units along the route of flight about any changes that have been made to the original FPL. In this example, flight BA456 has changed its route to include PTASO, UN491 and GIMER and has a new air traffic control identifier.

4. DLA (Delay Message)
The format for DLA message in AMHS is as follows:

MESSAGE TYPE: DLA
MESSAGE SEQUENCE NUMBER: 456789
MESSAGE TYPE ADDITIONAL INDICATORS: NIL
REFERENCE NUMBER: OPQ123
MESSAGE ORIGINATOR: EGLLZTZX
MESSAGE RECIPIENT: EDDFZTZX
TIME OF MESSAGE: 154500Z
FLIGHT ID: BA789
MESSAGE TEXT: 
DLA-BA789-EGLL
-DELAY/RUNWAY CLOSURE
-ETD/1605
-PBN/A1B2C3D4O1S2

DLA message is used to report any delay that is likely to affect the air traffic flow. In this example, flight BA789 is delayed due to runway closure and its estimated time of departure is now 1605.

5. CNL (Cancellation Message)
The format for CNL message in AMHS is as follows:

MESSAGE TYPE: CNL
MESSAGE SEQUENCE NUMBER: 567890
MESSAGE TYPE ADDITIONAL INDICATORS: NIL
REFERENCE NUMBER: RST678
MESSAGE ORIGINATOR: EGLLZTZX
MESSAGE RECIPIENT: EDDFZTZX
TIME OF MESSAGE: 161500Z
FLIGHT ID: BA123
MESSAGE TEXT: 
CNL-BA123-EGLL
-REASON/ENGINE FAILURE

The CNL message is used to inform the ATC units that a particular flight has been cancelled for a specific reason. In this example, flight BA123 is cancelled due to engine failure.
